name: Orforglipron
subtitle: Oral Small-Molecule GLP-1 Receptor Agonist | Weight Loss & Diabetes

modifications: Biased agonism profile favoring G protein activation over β-arrestin recruitment

overview: First oral non-peptide GLP-1 completing Phase 3 trials. Achieves substantial weight loss without injections, refrigeration, or dietary restrictions, with clinical evidence of 12.4% weight reduction at 72 weeks.
mechanism: Small-molecule GLP-1 receptor agonist with biased signaling preferentially activating G protein/cAMP pathways, enhancing insulin secretion, suppressing glucagon, delaying gastric emptying, and reducing appetite while minimizing receptor desensitization. 79.1% oral bioavailability with 29-49 hour half-life.
keyBenefits: Significant weight loss (up to 12.4% at 72 weeks)
No refrigeration or food restrictions required

items.name: Obesity Without Diabetes
items.description: ATTAIN-1 demonstrated 12.4% weight loss (27.3 lbs) at 72 weeks with 36mg dose; 59.6% achieving ≥10% weight loss.

items.name: Obesity With Type 2 Diabetes
items.description: ATTAIN-2 showed 10.5% weight loss with 72.8% achieving ≥5% weight loss.

items.name: Cardiometabolic Risk Reduction
items.description: Improvements in waist circumference, systolic blood pressure (8-12 mmHg), triglycerides (-20-30%).

items.name: Prediabetes Prevention
items.description: 91% achieved near-normal blood sugar levels versus 42% with placebo.

items.name: Type 2 Diabetes Management
items.description: ACHIEVE-1 Phase 3 trial showed HbA1c reductions of 1.3-1.6% from 8.0% baseline; 76.2% achieving HbA1c <7%.

items.name: Insulin Resistance Improvement
items.description: Significant improvements in insulin sensitivity indices within 4 weeks of therapy initiation.

deliveryMethods.overview: Once-daily oral tablet requiring no reconstitution. Can be taken with or without food or water at any time of day.
keyBenefits: Flexible dosing with or without meals

interactions.notes: Both are GLP-1 agonists; combining increases severe hypoglycemia and excessive GI adverse effects.
Dual GIP/GLP-1 agonist overlaps with GLP-1 mechanism; combination contraindicated.
Another GLP-1 agonist; concurrent use contraindicated due to overlapping mechanisms.
Orforglipron enhances insulin sensitivity; may require significant insulin dose reduction.
Complementary mechanisms for diabetes management; Phase 3 trials showed enhanced efficacy.
No known interactions; may support gut health and potentially mitigate GI adverse effects.

timeline.effects: Appetite reduction typically within 1-3 days; mild to moderate nausea common
Gradual weight loss begins (0.5-2 lbs per week); improved blood sugar control
Reduced food cravings; GI adverse effects generally diminish; metabolic markers improving
Continued progressive weight loss without plateau
Long-term therapy well-tolerated; sustained benefits at 72 weeks

common: Diarrhea (more prevalent than with injectable GLP-1s)
stopSigns: Severe or persistent abdominal pain radiating to back (potential pancreatitis)
Neck lumps, hoarseness, difficulty swallowing, or neck swelling
Severe nausea/vomiting preventing adequate nutrition or hydration
Signs of severe hypoglycemia (confusion, sweating, rapid heartbeat)
Suicidal thoughts, severe depression, or significant mood changes
Vision changes or persistent eye pain
contraindications: Personal or family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma
monitoring: Monitor for signs of acute pancreatitis
Adjust other diabetes medications to prevent hypoglycemia
Monitor for gallbladder disease symptoms with rapid weight loss
Watch for suicidal thoughts or significant mood changes

good: Intact tablet packaging with proper labeling, batch numbers, and expiration dates
Uniform tablet appearance without discoloration, chipping, or cracking
warning: As of late 2025, orforglipron remains investigational only; any product claiming FDA approval is fraudulent
bad: Discolored, crumbling, or damaged tablets indicate improper storage

references.title: ATTAIN-1 Phase 3 Trial (Obesity)
references.keyFindings: 3,127 adults; 72 weeks. 36mg dose achieved 12.4% weight loss (27.3 lbs) versus 2.1% placebo. First oral small-molecule GLP-1 completing Phase 3.

references.title: ATTAIN-2 Phase 3 Trial (Obesity + Type 2 Diabetes)
references.keyFindings: 1,613 adults; 72 weeks. 36mg dose achieved 10.5% weight loss with 72.8% achieving ≥5% weight loss.

references.title: ACHIEVE-1 Phase 3 Trial (Type 2 Diabetes)
references.keyFindings: 559 adults; 40 weeks. All doses significantly reduced HbA1c by 1.3-1.6%; 76.2% achieved HbA1c <7%.

references.title: Phase 2 Obesity Study
references.keyFindings: 272 adults; 36 weeks. Achieved up to 14.7% mean weight reduction at 36 weeks with 45mg dose.

quickStats.typicalDose: Start 3-6mg daily, titrate up to 12-36mg based on response
quickStats.cycleDuration: Long-term continuous therapy (72+ weeks in trials)
